key_evidence: UNGA formally adopted A/80/L.65 endorsing the ICJ climate advisory opinion 141-8-28 on May 21 with Vanuatu as lead co-sponsor — the explicit forward trigger from May 22 scan has fired
National Loss and Damage Policy domestication: 11 thematic areas, 85 directives, 11.7B Vatu 5-year plan with budget — international advocacy now translated into binding domestic law
Sustained INT-dimension governance translation pattern: international advocacy → multilateral outcome → domestic implementation, all within a 30-day window
AOSIS / Pacific climate leadership role corroborated by the resolution margin (141-8-28) — Tier-1 multilateral institutional confirmation
Cumulative delta +5.5 from published 35.9 (May 22 documented +3.5 plus +2.0 forward-trigger-fired uplift per new precedent)
methodology_novel: FORWARD-TRIGGER-RESOLUTION-CONFIRMED-FIRED — first benchmark case of a scanner-documented forward trigger firing in the same news cycle. Established standard protocol: promote sub-threshold accumulator to formal-apply review; add +1.5 to +2.5 uplift on top of accumulator depending on institutional weight (+2.0 for Tier-1 multilateral outcomes, +1.0 for Tier-2 single-state policy events). For Vanuatu: +3.5 documented accumulator + 2.0 Tier-1 fired-trigger uplift = +5.5 total.
